name: benjamin-franklin
description: Walter Isaacson's Benjamin Franklin — American biography tracing Franklin from runaway apprentice to printer, scientist, inventor, diplomat, and founding father, exploring how he invented the American character through practical wisdom and self-improvement
useCase: Understand the life of Benjamin Franklin, his scientific contributions, diplomatic achievements, and the self-improvement system that defined him
knownRisks: The book is sympathetic to Franklin and may underemphasize his flaws (slavery, treatment of his wife)
Isaacson is a journalist, not an academic historian — some scholars may prefer more depth
The skill condenses a 20,000-line biography significantly
